title: Türkiye - Ukraine Free Trade Agreement (FTA)
bodyHtml:
A bill regarding the approval of the Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between the Government of the Republic of Türkiye and the Government of Ukraine, and granting the authority to the President for the direct approval of the annexes of the said agreement, has been submitted to the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye.

The FTA is expected to come into effect upon its ratification by the President and the completion of other processes.

Key Provisions of the FTA:

Industrial Products:

Ukraine will eliminate customs duties on most industrial products, including automotive, textiles, ready-made garments, and chemical products, effective from the date the FTA comes into force. Additional concessions will be implemented by the end of the fifth year, making nearly all of Türkiye’s exports duty-free.

Türkiye will reduce customs duties on most industrial products for Ukraine by the end of the seventh year, with some products only receiving partial duty reductions or subject to quotas. Sensitive iron and steel products will be subject to quantity restrictions, with some products excluded from the FTA.

Agricultural Products:

Ukraine will eliminate customs duties on most agricultural imports from Türkiye once the FTA is in force. Competitive Turkish exports like tomatoes, peppers, cucumbers, grapes, pickles, canned tomatoes, fruit juices, hazelnuts, beans, lentils, apples, pears, quinces, apricots, cherries, and peaches will benefit from these duty eliminations.

For a few agricultural products, Ukraine will eliminate customs duties over a ten-year period.

Ukraine agreed to tariff quotas and duty reductions for some agricultural products. However, around half of Türkiye’s agricultural imports from Ukraine will remain outside the FTA. Notably, corn, sunflower oil, and wheat, where Ukraine is competitive, are excluded from concessions.

Animal Products:

Limited quotas are granted for some animal products (including certain dairy products, eggs, and poultry meat).

Customs duty reductions are agreed for dairy-cream-yogurt, margarine, and certain fruits and vegetables.

Additional Provisions:

With the FTA in force, Turkish exporters will gain competitive access to the Ukrainian market for textiles, ready-made garments, automotive, white goods, fresh fruits and vegetables, and processed agricultural products under conditions similar to those enjoyed by EU exporters.

Ukraine commits to not imposing export taxes on raw animal hides, copper, ingots, and other primary forms of stainless steel. For sunflower seeds, Ukraine will not impose customs duties on up to 50,000 tons exported to Türkiye.

For detailed explanations on the removal of duties, see from page 60 onwards.

-For staging categories for the elimination of customs duties by Turkey: Pages 170 to 263

- For staging categories for the elimination of customs duties by Ukraine: Pages 63 to 169

Products not listed in the respective sections will have their duties eliminated.

To access the full text of the Agreement, click here.
